description:
Advances: mutagen.cli

Deliverable:
- Wrap the existing `Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)` call at `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:236` in a private helper that recognises the archive-context failure tuple `{:error, {:mutagen_ex, {_, ~c"mutagen_ex.app"}}}` (and the `:non_existing` variant) and self-heals before raising.
- Self-heal sequence:
  1. `Mix.Local.append_archives/0` then retry `Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)`.
  2. If still erroring, glob `Path.join(Mix.path_for(:archives), "*")`, call `Mix.Local.archive_ebin/1` on each candidate, and `:code.add_pathz/1` the first ebin that contains `mutagen_ex.app`. Retry once more.
  3. If all three attempts fail, route to the structured-error path below.
- Structured-error path (replaces the raw `MatchError` crash): construct a `%MutagenEx.JsonReporter.Report{}` via `base_report(nil)`, populate `details: %{message: "..."}` with archive-install troubleshooting copy, invoke `MutagenEx.JsonReporter.emit_error(report, :runtime_load_failed)`, write to stdout via `MutagenEx.Pipeline.DefaultIo.emit(iodata, 2, %Config{json_path: nil})`, and halt with exit code 2. No new IO surface invented.
- Detection-predicate discipline: gate the repair branch ONLY on the failure-tuple shape returned by `Application.ensure_all_started/1`. Do NOT probe env vars, `Mix.Project.config/0`, or `:code.lib_dir/1` to "guess" archive context. The first attempt's success is the only signal that an existing-and-working adoption path is in use.
- Implementer choice point (document in a code comment): primary repair = `Mix.Local.append_archives/0` (intent-matching, documented in Mix); defensive fallback = the `archive_ebin/1` scan. Both APIs are public in Mix 1.19.5 / OTP 28. Either is sufficient alone if the other regresses across Mix versions; ship both.
- Unit-test seam: either `@doc false def __ensure_runtime__` or a process-dictionary failure override keyed `:mutagen_ensure_runtime_force_failure` (mirroring the existing `mutagen_json_path_project_root` override at line 507). Implementer picks based on what reads cleanest.

Spec touches:
- `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` — adds `mutagen.cli.r16`, scenarios `s14c` + `s14d`, verification `v11` (already authored by /distill on this branch — implementer flips `v11.execute: false` to `true` once S2's test file lands).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs --only archive_context` covering two named cases:
  1. `"ensure_runtime/0 succeeds when :mutagen_ex is already loaded (dep-adoption shape)"` — baseline; never enters repair branch.
  2. `"ensure_runtime/0 routes to structured-error JSON on unrecoverable load failure"` — uses the force-failure seam; asserts `aborted: true`, `abort_reason: "runtime_load_failed"`, exit code 2.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.cli.s14c` (archive context success after self-heal) and `mutagen.cli.s14d` (unrecoverable failure routes to structured JSON, no MatchError). The integration test that exercises s14c end-to-end is owned by S2 (think-pvr.2); S1's unit tests cover the structured-error path of s14d and the no-op-on-dep-path discipline.
- Mechanical: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix smoke` green.
- Spec validation: `mix spec.validate` green.

Out of Scope (files):
- `test/integration/archive_install_test.exs` (owned by S2)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex` (OTP app spec is correct — the bug is purely `:code` path visibility)
- `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` (owned by S3)
- Any other `lib/mutagen_ex/*.ex` modules (the repair lives in the Mix task and helpers it calls)
- `mix.exs` (S2 owns the one-line aliases edit)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex`
- `test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs`
- `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` (already partially authored by /distill — implementer may flip v11.execute from `false` to `true` if S2 ships in the same PR; otherwise leave for S2 to flip)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No new third-party deps. The repair uses `Mix.Local.append_archives/0`, `Mix.path_for(:archives)`, `Mix.Local.archive_ebin/1`, and `:code.add_pathz/1` — all stdlib / Mix-internal surface.
- No env-var or config flag gates the fix. Self-heal triggers on the failure tuple; user does nothing.
- No edits to `test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs` — its behaviour is the byte-for-byte parallel reference and must remain unchanged.
- Do NOT change the public surface of `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/1` or `run/2`. The repair lives behind `ensure_runtime/0`.
- Do NOT introduce a new abort_reason atom beyond `:runtime_load_failed` (and ensure the existing reason-naming convention in JsonReporter is followed).

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean
- [ ] Unit tests in `test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs` pass
- [ ] `mix spec.validate` green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: codex-9f1a2b34
Log: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-runtime-preamble-repair/.codex-runs/mutagen-wrd.40.1.log

Commit: 5a958ceb87f0cbc4f2d831cae6aa0e6e78e114e7
Files touched: lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex; test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs
Tests added: Mix.Tasks.MutagenTest "ensure_runtime/0 succeeds when :mutagen_ex is already loaded (dep-adoption shape)"; Mix.Tasks.MutagenTest "ensure_runtime/0 routes to structured-error JSON on unrecoverable load failure"
Verification stubs flipped: none

Summary: Added a hidden ensure-runtime seam plus archive-context repair behind the existing run/1 preamble. The first successful Application.ensure_all_started/1 result bypasses repair, archive-context failure tuples trigger append_archives plus targeted ebin fallback, and unrecoverable failures now emit runtime_load_failed JSON through the existing reporter/io path instead of raising MatchError.

title: S1: Runtime preamble repair + mutagen.cli.r16 (archive-context self-heal)
